title: Unclog a Drain
summary: Clear a slow or blocked sink, shower, or tub drain using simple tools and household methods instead of harsh chemicals.
learnerPromise: Clear a slow or blocked drain without harsh chemicals.
whyItMatters: Drains can get clogged with hair, soap, and food over time. Knowing how to clear them keeps water flowing and can save a call to a plumber.
realLifeUses: Clearing a bathroom sink that drains slowly
Fixing a shower drain clogged with hair
Helping a roommate or family member with a blocked tub
Preventing water from pooling in the sink
youWillLearn: How to safely remove a drain stopper or screen
How to use a simple tool to pull out clogs
How to flush the drain with hot water
When it is time to ask a plumber for help
miniChallenge: Find a sink or shower drain and identify the stopper or screen you would remove first.
steps: Look at the drain and find the stopper, screen, or cover on top.
Remove the cover carefully. Some twist off, some lift out, and some need a screwdriver.
Use a tool or your fingers to pull out visible hair or debris near the top.
Pour hot water down the drain to help flush loosened buildup.
Replace the cover and test the drain by running water.
completionCriteria: You identified how to remove the drain cover
You cleared visible debris from the drain opening
You tested the drain and water flowed better than before
commonProblems: The stopper will not come out — look for a small screw or tab holding it
The clog is deeper than you can reach — a simple drain snake may help
Water still backs up — this may need a plumber's help
tips: Wear gloves when pulling debris from drains
A simple zip-it tool or bent wire can grab hair clogs easily
Avoid pouring grease down kitchen sinks to prevent future clogs
helpBoundary: Call a plumber if multiple drains are clogged or water is backing up. Do not mix different drain cleaners.
